back_translation: Indeed, concerning the service to the saints, it is more than necessary for me to write to you.

For I know your intent, which I have taken pride in concerning you with the Macedonians, that the province of Achaia has been prepared since last year, and your zeal has stirred up most of them to rise.
But I sent those brothers, so that our pride concerning you in this matter would not become empty, but so that you would be prepared just as I had said.
Lest, if Macedonians come together with me and find that you are not prepared, we would be ashamed — to say nothing of you — in this confidence.

Therefore, I considered it necessary to ask the brothers to go to you in advance, in order to prepare in advance the blessing which you had previously promised, so that this blessing would be prepared in the manner of a blessing, and not in the manner of forced extortion through greed.
This is a matter to be remembered: namely, the one who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one who sows generously will also reap generously.
Let each one act according to what he has set in his heart, not with regret or with being forced, for God loves a giver with a cheerful heart.
And God is able to make all grace overflow to you, so that in every matter, every time, you will have all sufficiency, and overflow in every good action.
Just as it is written, 'He distributed; he gave to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.'
He who supplies seeds to the sower, and bread for food, will supply and increase the seeds of you, and will cause the produce of your righteousness to grow.
You will receive richness in every matter, in order to demonstrate generosity in every occasion, which through us this generosity also produces thanksgiving to God.
For the service in this ministry not only fills up the lack of the saints, but also overflows out into the thanksgiving of God from many people.

Through the proof of this service, they will give glory to God for the obedience in your acknowledgment of the gospel of Christ, and for the generosity in your participation toward them and toward all.
And they will plead for you in prayer, longing greatly for you, because of the great grace of God which is upon you.
Thanks be to God for his gift that cannot be described.
